title: Research Analysts (RA)
description:
Research Analysts prepare and publish research reports and recommendations on securities for general publication — not personalised advice. All Research Analysts in India must be registered with SEBI under the SEBI (Research Analysts) Regulations 2014.

The distinction between a Research Analyst and an Investment Adviser is important: RAs publish general research available to multiple clients, while IAs give personalised advice to specific individuals. Many fraudulent operators blur this line — operating as unregistered RAs while charging subscription fees for stock tips through Telegram channels and WhatsApp groups.

Pump and dump is the most common fraud involving fake RAs. Operators accumulate shares in a stock at low prices, then distribute fake research reports and tips promoting the stock, driving up the price. Once the price rises sufficiently, they sell their holdings — leaving retail investors who bought based on the fake tips with losses.
key_facts: All Research Analysts must be registered under SEBI (Research Analysts) Regulations 2014
RAs publish general research — not personalised investment advice
RAs must disclose any holdings in recommended securities
RAs must disclose if they received payment for publishing a report
Pump and dump using fake research is a SEBI enforcement priority
RAs cannot guarantee returns from their recommendations
Research reports must include mandatory risk disclosures
SEBI has taken action against hundreds of unregistered tip providers
red_flags: Research analyst not registered with SEBI
Sells stock tips for a fee without SEBI RA registration
Guarantees specific returns from stock recommendations
Tips distributed through WhatsApp or Telegram for subscription
Cannot provide SEBI RA registration number on request
Recommends penny stocks or illiquid stocks with unusual urgency
Only profitable past tips shown — losses never disclosed
Charges monthly subscription for stock tips
Does not disclose own holdings in recommended stocks
Tips always coincide with the analyst promoting the stock heavily
Uses AI generated fake research reports with professional formatting
Pressure to buy quickly before the tip becomes public
how_to_verify: Go to sebi.gov.in → Registers → Research Analysts
Search the analyst name or firm name
Verify SEBI RA registration number and validity
Check that research reports carry the SEBI registration number
Verify through BSE or NSE if analyst is empanelled
Check SEBI enforcement orders for any actions against the analyst
Verify disclosure of holdings in recommended stocks in the report
Call SEBI helpline 1800 266 7575 to verify RA registration
real_case: SEBI has taken enforcement action against multiple Telegram channel operators and WhatsApp group admins charging subscription fees for stock tips without SEBI RA registration. In several cases, operators were found to have taken positions in stocks before tipping them to subscribers — a classic pump and dump — profiting at the expense of their own subscribers.

quiz.question: What is the difference between a Research Analyst and an Investment Adviser?
options: There is no difference
RA publishes general research for multiple clients; IA gives personalised advice to specific individuals
RA works for institutions; IA works for retail investors only
RA charges lower fees than IA
quiz.explanation: RAs publish general research available broadly. IAs give personalised advice to specific clients. Both require separate SEBI registrations.

quiz.question: Under which regulation must Research Analysts be registered?
options: SEBI (Investment Advisers) Regulations 2013
SEBI (Research Analysts) Regulations 2014
SEBI (Portfolio Managers) Regulations 2020
SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations 1996
quiz.explanation: Research Analysts must be registered under SEBI (Research Analysts) Regulations 2014.

quiz.question: What is a pump and dump scheme?
options: A legitimate trading strategy using technical analysis
Operators buy stock, spread fake tips to inflate price, then sell — leaving retail investors with losses
A type of SEBI approved scheme
Day trading using borrowed funds
quiz.explanation: Pump and dump involves buying stock at low price, spreading fake tips to inflate it, then selling. Retail investors who buy based on the tips are left with losses.

quiz.question: A Telegram channel has been giving stock tips for 2 years and claims 85% accuracy. They have no SEBI registration. What should you do?
options: Subscribe — track record proves reliability
Subscribe for 1 month to test
Do not subscribe and report to SEBI
Subscribe but verify each tip independently
quiz.explanation: Operating a paid stock tips service without SEBI RA registration is illegal regardless of claimed track record. Report to SEBI.

quiz.question: What must a legitimate Research Analyst disclose in their research report?
options: Only their buy or sell recommendation
Their own holdings in the recommended security and any payments received for the report
Only their qualifications
Nothing — research is opinion and needs no disclosure
quiz.explanation: SEBI mandates disclosure of analyst holdings in recommended securities and any compensation received. This prevents conflicts of interest.

quiz.question: An operator buys shares of a small company, then sends you a research report strongly recommending that stock. This is most likely:
options: Genuine research — they invested because they believe in the stock
A pump and dump scheme — they will sell their shares after you and others buy
Standard market practice
A sign of conviction in the stock
quiz.explanation: This is a classic pump and dump pattern. The operator profits when your buying drives up the price, then sells leaving you with losses.

quiz.question: Can a SEBI registered Research Analyst guarantee returns on their recommendations?
options: Yes if they have more than 5 years track record
Yes for large cap stocks only
No — no RA can legally guarantee returns
Yes if the recommendation is for a short timeframe
quiz.explanation: No Research Analyst can legally guarantee returns. Markets are inherently uncertain and any return guarantee is a fraud signal.

quiz.question: What should you check in a research report to verify it is from a legitimate SEBI registered RA?
options: Quality of the report formatting
SEBI Research Analyst registration number on the report
Number of pages in the report
Whether the analyst has appeared on TV
quiz.explanation: All legitimate research reports must carry the SEBI RA registration number. Verify this number on SEBI website.

quiz.question: A research report shows that the analyst's 10 previous recommendations all gave profits. What is the most important question to ask?
options: What are the next 10 recommendations
Were losses on any recommendations also disclosed — cherry picking only profitable tips is misleading
How long each trade took to reach target
How many subscribers the analyst has
quiz.explanation: Showing only profitable tips without disclosing losses is misleading. SEBI regulations require fair and balanced disclosure of track record.

quiz.question: Unregistered operators offering paid stock tips through Telegram or WhatsApp in India are:
options: Operating in a regulatory grey area
Allowed if they add standard disclaimers
Violating SEBI Research Analysts Regulations and subject to enforcement action
Allowed for educational purposes
quiz.explanation: Operating a paid stock tips service without SEBI RA registration violates SEBI Regulations. SEBI has taken multiple enforcement actions against such operators.
